item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment procedures has unnecessary detail, does it breach containment if the cell is not that specific size? No, it doesn't.
Also, there's too much pointless redaction. I know it's revealed in the incident log, it's pointless.
I like the tests and incident log, but I can't upvote this.
No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nowadays, probably yeah, but back when this was written, mass redaction was still the trend. We were starting to pull out of it as a community, and so I wanted to make an article where the redacted info was still available to the reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test subject showed great interest in climbing research assistant's trouser leg. Showed no concern for object. When placed atop it, subject began vocalizing distress after two minutes, due to difficulty in reaching the floor. Test stopped and subject recovered.

Aww

I loved this SCP, but I couldn't help but think two things:
-Why is the addendum just a continuation of the description? Shouldn't that be in the description?
-The researcher's note at the end kind of spoils what happens in the incident log.

It's still good enough to warrant my upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand that all the redacted information is available in the incident log, I just don't understand why it was redacted in the first place. Why do you have to be level 3 to be told that the 1381-02s look like the big cats that went in, only able to talk? How does it benefit the containment of this item to keep people who might have to clean up after it from knowing that if some idiot manages to get clearance to throw another tiger in, they're going to end up with a swarm of hellcats led by Shere Khan instead of just a swarm of hellcats?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you read the whole thing? All the redacted info shows up in the incident log. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to upvote but the 4 redactions in 2 sentences really put me off, certainly not going to downvote though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it just doesnt seem like something the foundation would do to me. "This things knocking, lets just open it" it might be good if it played out differently, but there was also not enough description for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmmmm. It's OK. I liked the first bit, where the cats are mysteriously very keen on the cabinet, I winced hugely at the bleeding paws, and I liked the kitten, but the hellcats just seemed a huge disappointment. They didn't really do anything.

Oh, also, were I a researcher at the Foundation, I'd be that guy that gets a reprimand for causing huge facility damage trying to destroy this thing. Can't abide things that hurt cats! Poor paws!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wanted to downvote for kitty killing, but i actually like this. But since when to cats need magic cabinets to become hellspawn?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was intrigued by this, and the redactions were explaine later on (thankfully). I do agree with what Roget said, though. It's just a curiosity, and this can be seen in the old discussion on this page. I thought it was interesting enough, though, so no vote for now.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This article is up for review. Read it and weep.

I feel like this is missing some central element. Like, I like all the individual bits of the effect, and I kinda follow where it's going, but then it just sorta stops without any real resolution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm confused as to why the Foundation hasn't kept a few giant talking kitties around to interview/study.

My confusion has nothing to do with how cool I think giant talking kitties would be. NOTHING at ALL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the redacted information is contained in the collapsibles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1381-02 are [REDACTED], luminescent eyes and elongated claws similar to SCP-1381-01, but lacking the dark pigmentation. SCP-1381-02 are significantly larger than [REDACTED], and display increased speed, strength, endurance and [REDACTED]. SCP-1381-02 are capable of directing SCP-1381-01 via [REDACTED].

Ehhh, think you could put another [REDACTED] there? I thik the first line could use one more…
No, for real, that part feels clunky, and lazy. Like you didn't wanna think what to add, and shoved redacteds so we filled the blanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, the -2's lack the dark pigmentation. That's specified, though easy to miss. So the little ones are black and extra pointy, the big ones look like they did when they went in, but with some modifications.

Nice to see a little late-game feedback on this. That warmed my heart. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually rather like this. My one big problem is this: If the cats produced by the cabinet reflect no light… then it'd be awfully difficult for them to have similar patterning to the cats put in. Maybe change that bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Redactions explained
Spoilers!

The first two refer to SCP-1381-02 looking like the pantherine test subjects, the second is intelligence and the third speech and tactical ability.

I figure this stuff is not something they want getting out. It is very likely, however, that I am doing redaction wrong, and misunderstanding why it's used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I get from the incident log is that the first two [REDACTED]s are "panther" and the third is either "intelligence" or "speech", and the fourth is probably "speech". I don't see why any of that would need level 3 clearance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Compiled a bunch of the feedback I've gotten and made changes to various things. Doesn't feel like a huge difference, but perhaps it is enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This can only lead to heartbreak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm considering just leaving out the chain-wrapping thing. It was a last-minute addition and may not be worth it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cats fucking love going into cabinets on any day that ends in Y. If that's your hook, it's not going to work.

We've got cat themed horror on the wiki (such as SCP-511) and this just isn't it, bro.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha. Maybe add in a thing about how everyone exposed to it becomes allergic to cats and bump it up to keter XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also didn't like the phrase "buy extra time". Perhaps "slow containment breach"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it might be more effective if the cats didn't come back out… yet. or perhaps not in the same place. something that leaves room for the horror of implication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the fact that the cats want to go into the thing? That's my only defense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I obviously did not do enough research, so thank you. I will implement all of this and most likely change out the lynx for a pantherine once I've compiled all the feedback. Many thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a drawer that absorbs cats and then cats come out but the cats are different and some cats order the other cats around and it bends to absorb bigger cats and these cats can talk and it might be 'saving' cats to launch at people.

Eeeuugh. Am I missing anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is neither interesting or frightening. I can't see any way to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
short version: felines are small cats, felids are ALL cats. longer version: big cats are pantherines, and necessarily not felines (pantherinae and felinae are the two subfamilies of family felidae). small cats are everything that isn't a lion, tiger, jaguar, or leopard of some sort - pumas, lynxes, cheetahs, cervals, anything with "cat" at the end are felines.

in the main part of the article, i would suggest replacing all but the third instance of "feline" with "felid," and substituting "pantherines" for "Larger felines". the three instances in the test log are correct, though you might want to test a young pantherine for thoroughness. also, i *personally* would make the lynx behave like a house cat, rather than a big cat, but that's just me trying to make the behavioral distinction phylogenetically tidy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it wasn't just me then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So one day I saw my cat digging at the side of our dresser, like he so often does, and wondered to myself, "Why in the hell does he do that?"

To answer the question, my brain went, "He wants inside. Why does he want inside? So that he can become a demonic hellbeast and wreak unholy vengeance on his oppressors. SCP it!"

Then this happened. I may not have gotten all of that across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those items that just is. A dresser that attracts cats. Why? Because who the fuck knows, that's why.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think you may be right. I promise not to do the "Safe Euclid" thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mostly just wanted to show that they can talk. I could probably suggest the same in the incident log though, now that you mention it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I was really tired when I wrote that. What I meant was; the object actively influences things outside of its containment, and the procedures mention that it can't actually be contained during certain events—both of these are characteristics of Euclids. A safe class wouldn't cause any problems even if it weren't locked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I'm not sure why they became Displacer Beasts. I mean, they aren't, but yeah.

All the redactions are included in the incident log in some form or another. I figured, if you need high security clearance to read those parts, it makes sense that there's stuff in there they don't want low-security people knowing about. Does that make sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, and I'll take that under heavy consideration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you lock it in a room cats can't get into, nothing happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad that didn't kill the tone. I included that test just so no one would go "ugh demon kittens nothx".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, felid. Would you be able to explain just what the proper usage between the two is? Not my field, unfortunately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good. Interesting. Though I wonder "why cats".
Something about it isn't quite catching me.
I am on the line between no vote and +1.

So no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't mind this. The interview is too similar to 682 for my liking, and therefore fundamentally pointless. Otherwise, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Being a significant D&D nerd, I read the description of 1381-1 and instantly thought "Displacer Beast spawner". But then I got the description of -2 and I winced. FAR too much redaction, all we get is that it's… Bigger than something. And is somehow able to control the others. Oh and it can talk, but you don't find that out until the interview logs.

That said, I actually kinda-sorta like this one, especially the fact that the desk is apparently not invincible, but there's some sort of effect going on that not only accommodates cats of various sizes, but makes damn sure well that those doors WILL open and WILL shut, beyond pine's normal stress tolerances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone's good, but I'm not sure how to feel about the object. It basically is a 682-but-cats-making machine. Also, no official document should include the phrase "buy extra time."

That being said, I did get a laugh out of "Test subject showed great interest in climbing research assistant's trouser leg."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I don't particularly like the idea of a giant wooden cat magnet, I do find it interesting. I would only suggest perhaps removing the attribute of an expanding area of effect. Something about appeasing the dresser with kitten sacrifices doesn't strike my fancy.

But it is well written for a first go!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a safe-class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like it, though it feels a bit overpowered - the effect is intriguing, i just don't like the "you cannot hope to contain it, you can only hope to slow it down" bit about chains and spikes and slowly growing radius of attraction.

also - i think a number of references to "felines" should more properly be "felids".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first outing. A weird idea that I wrote up as soon as it came to me. Hopefully a good first, since I'm not married to it in the least. Thanks to everyone on the forums who gave me feedback.

Cry havoc and release the dogs of war.
